author: peter.davies
content:
The current situation is that a small number of users create ephemeral contracts and then selfdestruct them. There is no economic benefit to doing so, these users pay the full gas fees to create the contract and then pay a further 5000 gas to selfdestruct it. There is no good reason for anyone to call selfdestruct and the only reason to retain it is that code that relies on it will break.

Independently, we may wish to intentionally support ephemeral contracts. The SELFDESTRUCT opcode is not a sensible vehicle for this. Instead we should start from clean sheet design, presumably adding a `TCREATE` opcode that creates a contract that will only exist until the end of the current transaction. The gas discounts that `TCREATE` could offer over `CREATE` are substantial.

These two projects can be pursued separately. Neither needs to wait for the other.
